text_original: : Motse said: The interest of the wise (ruler) lies in carrying out what makes for order among the people and avoiding what makes for confusion.
text_normalized: : Motse said: The interest of the wise (ruler) lies in carrying out what makes for order among the people and avoiding what makes for confusion.

text_original: But what is it that makes for order among the people?
text_normalized: But what is it that makes for order among the people?

text_original: When the administration of the ruler answers to the desires of the people there will be order, otherwise there will be confusion.
text_normalized: When the administration of the ruler answers to the desires of the people there will be order, otherwise there will be confusion.

text_original: How do we know it is so?
text_normalized: How do we know it is so?

text_original: When the administration of the ruler answers to the desires of the subjects, it manifests an imderstanduig of the approvals and disapprovals of the people.
text_normalized: When the administration of the ruler answers to the desires of the subjects, it manifests an imderstanduig of the approvals and disapprovals of the people.

text_original: When there is such an under-standing, the good will be discovered and rewarded and the bad will be discovered and punished, and the country will surely have order.
text_normalized: When there is such an under-standing, the good will be discovered and rewarded and the bad will be discovered and punished, and the country will surely have order.

text_original: When the administration of the ruler does not answer to the desires of the subjects, it shows a lack of understanding of the approvals and disapprovals of the subjects.
text_normalized: When the administration of the ruler does not answer to the desires of the subjects, it shows a lack of understanding of the approvals and disapprovals of the subjects.

text_original: When there is no such understanding then the good will not be discovered and rewarded and the bad will not be discovered and punished.
text_normalized: When there is no such understanding then the good will not be discovered and rewarded and the bad will not be discovered and punished.

text_original: With the good unrewarded and the evil unpunished, such a government will surely put the country into disorder.
text_normalized: With the good unrewarded and the evil unpunished, such a government will surely put the country into disorder.

text_original: Therefore when rewards and punishments do not answer to the desires of the people, the matter has to be carefully looked into.
text_normalized: Therefore when rewards and punishments do not answer to the desires of the people, the matter has to be carefully looked into.

text_original: But how can the desires of the people (being so many and various) be met?
text_normalized: But how can the desires of the people (being so many and various) be met?

text_original: Therefore Motse said: It can be done only by adopting the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: It can be done only by adopting the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.

text_original: How do we know the principle of Identification with the Superior can govern the empire?
text_normalized: How do we know the principle of Identification with the Superior can govern the empire?

text_original: ■Why not then examine the administration and the theory of government of the ancient times?
text_normalized: ■Why not then examine the administration and the theory of government of the ancient times?

text_original: In the beginning there was no ruler and everybody was independent.
text_normalized: In the beginning there was no ruler and everybody was independent.

text_original: Since every one was independent, there would be one purpose when there was one man, ten purposes when there were ten men, a hundred purposes when there were a hundred men, a thousand purposes when there were a thousand men and so on until the number of men became innumerable and the number of different purposes became innumerable with it.
text_normalized: Since every one was independent, there would be one purpose when there was one man, ten purposes when there were ten men, a hundred purposes when there were a hundred men, a thousand purposes when there were a thousand men and so on until the number of men became innumerable and the number of different purposes became innumerable with it.

text_original: And all of them approved their own ideas and disapproved those of others.
text_normalized: And all of them approved their own ideas and disapproved those of others.

text_original: And there was strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.
text_normalized: And there was strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven wished to unify the standards in the world.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven wished to unify the standards in the world.

text_original: The virtuous was selected and made emperor.
text_normalized: The virtuous was selected and made emperor.

text_original: conscious of the insufficiency of his power alone to govern the empire, the emperor chose the next best (in virtue and wisdom) and honoured them to be the three ministers.
text_normalized: conscious of the insufficiency of his power alone to govern the empire, the emperor chose the next best (in virtue and wisdom) and honoured them to be the three ministers.

text_original: Conscious of the insuflficiency of their powers alone to assist the emperor, the three ministers in turn divided the empire into feudal states and assigned them to feudal lords.
text_normalized: Conscious of the insuflficiency of their powers alone to assist the emperor, the three ministers in turn divided the empire into feudal states and assigned them to feudal lords.

text_original: Conscious of the insufficiency of his power alone to govern all that were within his four borders, the feudal lord in turn selected his next beat and commissioned them ministers and secretaries.
text_normalized: Conscious of the insufficiency of his power alone to govern all that were within his four borders, the feudal lord in turn selected his next beat and commissioned them ministers and secretaries.

text_original: Conscious of the insufficiency of their power alone to assist their feudal lord, the ministers and secretaries again selected their next best and appointed them district heads and clan patriarchs.
text_normalized: Conscious of the insufficiency of their power alone to assist their feudal lord, the ministers and secretaries again selected their next best and appointed them district heads and clan patriarchs.

text_original: lords, the ministers and secretaries, and the district heads and clan patriarchs, the emperor was not selecting them for wealth and honour, leisure and ease.
text_normalized: lords, the ministers and secretaries, and the district heads and clan patriarchs, the emperor was not selecting them for wealth and honour, leisure and ease.

text_original: It was to employ them to help in administration and jurisdiction.
text_normalized: It was to employ them to help in administration and jurisdiction.

text_original: Hence, when Heaven established the empire and located the.capital and commissioned the sovereign, kings, lords, and dukes, and appointed secretaries, scholars, professors, and elders — it was not to give them ease, but only to divide up the task and let them help carry out the light of Heaven.
text_normalized: Hence, when Heaven established the empire and located the.capital and commissioned the sovereign, kings, lords, and dukes, and appointed secretaries, scholars, professors, and elders — it was not to give them ease, but only to divide up the task and let them help carry out the light of Heaven.

text_original: Why are the superiors now imable to govern their sub-ordinates, and the subordinates unwilling to serve their superiors 1 It is because of a mutual disregard.
text_normalized: Why are the superiors now imable to govern their sub-ordinates, and the subordinates unwilling to serve their superiors 1 It is because of a mutual disregard.

text_original: What is the reason for this?
text_normalized: What is the reason for this?

text_original: The reason is a difference in standards.
text_normalized: The reason is a difference in standards.

text_original: Whenever standards differ there will be opposition.
text_normalized: Whenever standards differ there will be opposition.

text_original: The ruler may think a man good and reward him.
text_normalized: The ruler may think a man good and reward him.

text_original: The man, though rewarded by the ruler, yet by the same act provokes the condemnation of the people.
text_normalized: The man, though rewarded by the ruler, yet by the same act provokes the condemnation of the people.

text_original: Therefore those who do good are not necessarily encouraged by rewards.
text_normalized: Therefore those who do good are not necessarily encouraged by rewards.

text_original: The ruler may think a man evil and punish him.
text_normalized: The ruler may think a man evil and punish him.

text_original: This man, though punished by the ruler, yet at the same time receives the approval of the people.
text_normalized: This man, though punished by the ruler, yet at the same time receives the approval of the people.

text_original: Therefore those who do evil are not necessarily obstructed by punishments.
text_normalized: Therefore those who do evil are not necessarily obstructed by punishments.

text_original: Thus reward and honour from the ruler cannot encourage the good and his denunciation and punishment cannot prevent the evil.
text_normalized: Thus reward and honour from the ruler cannot encourage the good and his denunciation and punishment cannot prevent the evil.

text_original: What is the reason for this?
text_normalized: What is the reason for this?

text_original: The reason is a difference in standards.
text_normalized: The reason is a difference in standards.

text_original: But how can the standards in the world be unified?
text_normalized: But how can the standards in the world be unified?

text_original: Motse said: Why not let each member of the clan organize his purposes and identify them with those of the patriarch?
text_normalized: Motse said: Why not let each member of the clan organize his purposes and identify them with those of the patriarch?

text_original: whoever discovers a malefactor to the clan shall report it.
text_normalized: whoever discovers a malefactor to the clan shall report it.

text_original: Whoever reports the benefactor of the clan upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the clan himself.
text_normalized: Whoever reports the benefactor of the clan upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the clan himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the group will praise him.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the group will praise him.

text_original: Whoever fails to report a malefactor of the clan upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the clan himself.
text_normalized: Whoever fails to report a malefactor of the clan upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the clan himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of him the group will condemn him." Thereupon all the members of the clan wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their superior.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of him the group will condemn him." Thereupon all the members of the clan wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their superior.

text_original: Seeing the good they will report; seeing the evil they will report.
text_normalized: Seeing the good they will report; seeing the evil they will report.

text_original: And the patriarch can reward the good and punish the evil.
text_normalized: And the patriarch can reward the good and punish the evil.

text_original: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the clan will surely have order.
text_normalized: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the clan will surely have order.

text_original: Now, why is it that the clan becomes orderly?
text_normalized: Now, why is it that the clan becomes orderly?

text_original: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.
text_normalized: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.

text_original: Now that the clan is in order, is that all there is of the way of governing the feudal state?
text_normalized: Now that the clan is in order, is that all there is of the way of governing the feudal state?

text_original: By no means.
text_normalized: By no means.

text_original: The state is composed of many clans.
text_normalized: The state is composed of many clans.

text_original: They all like their own clan and dislike other clans.
text_normalized: They all like their own clan and dislike other clans.

text_original: And there is strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.
text_normalized: And there is strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.

text_original: Therefore the clan patriarchs should again organize the purposes in the clan and identify them with those of the feudal lord.
text_normalized: Therefore the clan patriarchs should again organize the purposes in the clan and identify them with those of the feudal lord.

text_original: The feudal lord also should give laws and should proclaim to the state: Whoever discovers a benefactor of the state shall report it; whoever discovers a malefactor of the state shall report it.
text_normalized: The feudal lord also should give laws and should proclaim to the state: Whoever discovers a benefactor of the state shall report it; whoever discovers a malefactor of the state shall report it.

text_original: Whoever reports a benefactor of the state upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the state himself.
text_normalized: Whoever reports a benefactor of the state upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the state himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the people will praise him.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the people will praise him.

text_original: Whoever fails to report a male-factor of the state upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the state himself.
text_normalized: Whoever fails to report a male-factor of the state upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the state himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of liim the people will condemn him." Thereupon all people in the state wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their superior.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of liim the people will condemn him." Thereupon all people in the state wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their superior.

text_original: Seeing the good they will report, seeing the evil they will report.
text_normalized: Seeing the good they will report, seeing the evil they will report.

text_original: And the feudal lord can rewmrd the good and punish the evil.
text_normalized: And the feudal lord can rewmrd the good and punish the evil.

text_original: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the feudal state will surely have order.
text_normalized: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the feudal state will surely have order.

text_original: Now, why is it that the state becomes orderly?
text_normalized: Now, why is it that the state becomes orderly?

text_original: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.
text_normalized: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.

text_original: Now that the feudal state is in order, is that all there is to the way of governing the empire?
text_normalized: Now that the feudal state is in order, is that all there is to the way of governing the empire?

text_original: By no means.
text_normalized: By no means.

text_original: The empire is composed of many states.
text_normalized: The empire is composed of many states.

text_original: They all like their own state and dislike other states.
text_normalized: They all like their own state and dislike other states.

text_original: And there is strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.
text_normalized: And there is strife among the strong and struggle among the weak.

text_original: Therefore the feudal lord should again organize the purposes in the state and identify them with those of the emperor.
text_normalized: Therefore the feudal lord should again organize the purposes in the state and identify them with those of the emperor.

text_original: The emperor also should give laws and should proclaim to the empire: Whoever discovers a benefactor of the empire shall report it; whoever discovers a malefactor of the empire shall report it.
text_normalized: The emperor also should give laws and should proclaim to the empire: Whoever discovers a benefactor of the empire shall report it; whoever discovers a malefactor of the empire shall report it.

text_original: Whoever reports a benefactor of the empire upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the state himself.
text_normalized: Whoever reports a benefactor of the empire upon seeing one is equivalent to benefiting the state himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the people will praise him.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will reward him, hearing of him the people will praise him.

text_original: Whoever fails to report a malefactor upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the empire himself.
text_normalized: Whoever fails to report a malefactor upon seeing one is equivalent to doing evil to the empire himself.

text_original: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of him the people will condemn him." Thereupon all the people in the empire will wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their emperor.
text_normalized: Knowing him the superior will punish him, hearing of him the people will condemn him." Thereupon all the people in the empire will wish to obtain reward and honour and avoid denunciation and punishment from their emperor.

text_original: Seeing the good and the evil they will report.
text_normalized: Seeing the good and the evil they will report.

text_original: And the emperor can reward the good and punish the evil.
text_normalized: And the emperor can reward the good and punish the evil.

text_original: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the empire will surely have order.
text_normalized: With the good rewarded and the evil punished, the empire will surely have order.

text_original: Now why is it that the empire becomes orderly?
text_normalized: Now why is it that the empire becomes orderly?

text_original: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.
text_normalized: Just because the administration is based on the principle of Identification with the Superior.

text_original: Now that the empire becomes orderly, the emperor will further organize the purposes in the empire and identify them with the Will of Heaven.
text_normalized: Now that the empire becomes orderly, the emperor will further organize the purposes in the empire and identify them with the Will of Heaven.

text_original: Therefore Identification with the Superior as a principle can govern the empire when used by the emperor, it can govern the state when used by the feudal lord, and it can govern the clan when used by the clan patriarch.
text_normalized: Therefore Identification with the Superior as a principle can govern the empire when used by the emperor, it can govern the state when used by the feudal lord, and it can govern the clan when used by the clan patriarch.

text_original: To be found not wanting when used on a large scale to govern the empire, and not useless when employed on a small scale to govern a clan — this is said of such a principle.
text_normalized: To be found not wanting when used on a large scale to govern the empire, and not useless when employed on a small scale to govern a clan — this is said of such a principle.

text_original: Hence the proverb: To govern the world-empire is the same as to rule a single family clan; to command all the people in the world is the same as to order a single individual.’ Does any one think that all this is just a fancy of Motse, and that this teaching did not exist among the sage-kings of old?
text_normalized: Hence the proverb: To govern the world-empire is the same as to rule a single family clan; to command all the people in the world is the same as to order a single individual.’ Does any one think that all this is just a fancy of Motse, and that this teaching did not exist among the sage-kings of old?

text_original: Really they were of the same opinion.
text_normalized: Really they were of the same opinion.

text_original: All the sage-kings administered their government by the principle of Identification with the Superior, therefore the world became orderly.
text_normalized: All the sage-kings administered their government by the principle of Identification with the Superior, therefore the world became orderly.

text_original: How do we know it is so?
text_normalized: How do we know it is so?

text_original: It is recorded in the Grand Oath " among the books of the ancient kings: If an unscrupulous man discovers a case of intrigue and deception and fails to make it known, he shall l)e punished equally." This is to say that whoever discovers any crime and does not report it will be taken ■ as committing a crime of the same order.
text_normalized: It is recorded in the Grand Oath " among the books of the ancient kings: If an unscrupulous man discovers a case of intrigue and deception and fails to make it known, he shall l)e punished equally." This is to say that whoever discovers any crime and does not report it will be taken ■ as committing a crime of the same order.

text_original: V, Therefore in governing the empire, the ancient sage-kings chose only the excellent for the outposts as well as for the offices near him.
text_normalized: V, Therefore in governing the empire, the ancient sage-kings chose only the excellent for the outposts as well as for the offices near him.

text_original: As there were many, to help him see and hear, he succeeded before others in planning, and completed before others in executing, and his good name -was spread before others.
text_normalized: As there were many, to help him see and hear, he succeeded before others in planning, and completed before others in executing, and his good name -was spread before others.

text_original: Just because he could trust Ms staS in the administration, the benefits were as we have stated.
text_normalized: Just because he could trust Ms staS in the administration, the benefits were as we have stated.

text_original: There is an ancient proverb saying: The sight of one eye cannot compare with that of two; the hearing of one ear cannot compare with that of two; the grasp of one hand cannot compare with that of two." Now, just because he could trust his staff in the administration the sage-king received such benefits.
text_normalized: There is an ancient proverb saying: The sight of one eye cannot compare with that of two; the hearing of one ear cannot compare with that of two; the grasp of one hand cannot compare with that of two." Now, just because he could trust his staff in the administration the sage-king received such benefits.

text_original: Therefore during the reign of the ancient sage-king over the empire, if there was a virtuous man more than a thousand li away he could reward him before the people in the same district and village all got to know it.
text_normalized: Therefore during the reign of the ancient sage-king over the empire, if there was a virtuous man more than a thousand li away he could reward him before the people in the same district and village all got to know it.

text_original: And if there was a wicked man about a thousand li away he could punish him before the people in the same district and village all got to know it.
text_normalized: And if there was a wicked man about a thousand li away he could punish him before the people in the same district and village all got to know it.

text_original: Though it may he supposed that the sage-king was keen in hearing and sight, how could he see all that is beyond a thousand li at one look, how could he hear all that is beyond a thousand li at one hearing?
text_normalized: Though it may he supposed that the sage-king was keen in hearing and sight, how could he see all that is beyond a thousand li at one look, how could he hear all that is beyond a thousand li at one hearing?

text_original: In fact the sage-king could see without going there and hear without being near.
text_normalized: In fact the sage-king could see without going there and hear without being near.

text_original: Yet what kept the thieves, robbers, bandits, and high-waymen moving all over the empire without being able to find refuge anywhere?
text_normalized: Yet what kept the thieves, robbers, bandits, and high-waymen moving all over the empire without being able to find refuge anywhere?

text_original: There is the beauty of adopting the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.
text_normalized: There is the beauty of adopting the principle of Identification with the Superior in government.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: Whoever orders his people to identify themselves with their superior must love them dearly.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: Whoever orders his people to identify themselves with their superior must love them dearly.

text_original: For the people will not obey orders except when they are ordered with love and held in confidence.
text_normalized: For the people will not obey orders except when they are ordered with love and held in confidence.

text_original: Lead them with wealth and honour ahead, and push them with just punishments from behind.
text_normalized: Lead them with wealth and honour ahead, and push them with just punishments from behind.

text_original: When government is carried on like this, even though I wanted to have some one not to identify himself with me, it would be impossible.
text_normalized: When government is carried on like this, even though I wanted to have some one not to identify himself with me, it would be impossible.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the kings, dukes, and important personages of the world now sincerely want to practise magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, if they want to attain the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and contribute toward the benefit of the people on the other, they cannot leave the principle of Identification with the Superior unexamined and un-understood: Identification with the Superior is, indeed, the foundation of government and essence of orderliness.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the kings, dukes, and important personages of the world now sincerely want to practise magnanimity and righteousness and be superior men, if they want to attain the way of the sage-kings on the one hand and contribute toward the benefit of the people on the other, they cannot leave the principle of Identification with the Superior unexamined and un-understood: Identification with the Superior is, indeed, the foundation of government and essence of orderliness.
